verses.text: 'Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise'; the king's terror is like a lion's roar; 'it is an honour for a man to cease from strife,' but every fool will be meddling. Drink deceives. To leave off quarrelling is honour, not weakness.

'Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise'; the king's terror is like a lion's roar; 'it is an honour for a man to cease from strife,' but every fool will be meddling. Drink deceives. To leave off quarrelling is honour, not weakness.

'Wine is a mocker, strong drink is raging: and whosoever is deceived thereby is not wise'; the king's terror is like a lion's roar; 'it is an honour for a man to cease from strife,' but every fool will be meddling. Drink deceives. To leave off quarrelling is honour, not weakness.

The sluggard will not plough for cold, so he begs in harvest; counsel in the heart is like deep water, but a man of understanding draws it out; most men proclaim their goodness, 'but a faithful man who can find?' Faithfulness is rare. The wise draw out hidden counsel; the lazy reap nothing.

The sluggard will not plough for cold, so he begs in harvest; counsel in the heart is like deep water, but a man of understanding draws it out; most men proclaim their goodness, 'but a faithful man who can find?' Faithfulness is rare. The wise draw out hidden counsel; the lazy reap nothing.

The sluggard will not plough for cold, so he begs in harvest; counsel in the heart is like deep water, but a man of understanding draws it out; most men proclaim their goodness, 'but a faithful man who can find?' Faithfulness is rare. The wise draw out hidden counsel; the lazy reap nothing.

'The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him'; a king on the throne of judgment scatters evil with his eyes; 'who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?' None is sinless. The integrity of a father blesses his children.

'The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him'; a king on the throne of judgment scatters evil with his eyes; 'who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?' None is sinless. The integrity of a father blesses his children.

'The just man walketh in his integrity: his children are blessed after him'; a king on the throne of judgment scatters evil with his eyes; 'who can say, I have made my heart clean, I am pure from my sin?' None is sinless. The integrity of a father blesses his children.

Diverse weights and measures are alike abomination to the LORD; even a child is known by his doings; 'the hearing ear, and the seeing eye, the LORD hath made even both of them.' God hates dishonest dealing. The faculties we use are His gift, accountable to Him.

'Love not sleep, lest thou come to poverty'; the buyer disparages, then boasts of his bargain; gold and rubies there are, but 'the lips of knowledge are a precious jewel.' Diligence guards against want. Wise speech is more precious than any gem.

Take a man's garment who is surety for a stranger; 'bread of deceit is sweet to a man; but afterwards his mouth shall be filled with gravel'; every purpose is established by counsel. Ill-gotten gain turns bitter. Wise plans need careful counsel.

A talebearer reveals secrets, so meddle not with the flatterer; whoso curses father or mother, his lamp shall be put out; an inheritance gotten hastily shall not be blessed. Honour your parents. What is grasped greedily and early seldom prospers.

'Say not thou, I will recompense evil; but wait on the LORD, and he shall save thee'; diverse weights are abomination; 'man's goings are of the LORD; how can a man then understand his own way?' Leave vengeance to God. Our steps are ordered by Him, beyond our understanding.

It is a snare to vow rashly and inquire later; a wise king scatters the wicked; 'the spirit of man is the candle of the LORD, searching all the inward parts.' Beware rash vows. God's light searches the deepest places of every heart.

'Mercy and truth preserve the king: and his throne is upholden by mercy'; the glory of young men is their strength, and the beauty of old men their grey head; stripes and blows cleanse away evil. Mercy upholds rule. Even painful correction can purge the inner man.
